name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (60g protein)
description: The house chocolate-chip cookie of record - the browned-butter protein cookie loaded to 60 g protein across 3 cookies (the high-protein build we keep going forward; the 50 g build was retired). Browned butter + a little real brown sugar for chew, whey + pudding mix for a soft set; baked low at 330°F for ~12–15 min and pulled pale so the centers stay gooey, with an optional pan-slap halfway for a flatter, chewier crinkle. Rated excellent.

payload.text: The 30-min chill firms the butter so the mounds hold their height and spread slowly — skip it and 60 g of whey in a small yield will spread flat and bake through before centers can stay gooey.
Let the browned butter cool a couple minutes before adding eggs — 60 g of whey is already heat-sensitive; scrambled egg in the fat ruins the emulsion and makes the cookie grainy.
60 g whey is a heavy protein load for 3 cookies; add milk a teaspoon at a time only if the dough won't come together — too much liquid makes it spread flat and bake rubbery.

payload.instructionText: Bake at 330°F / 165°C for ~12–15 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft - they stay pale, so don't wait for golden tops. Optional, for flatter, chewier cookies with crinkly tops: around the halfway mark (~7 min) slide the tray out and rap it firmly on the counter once or twice to deflate and spread the mounds (and again right out of the oven); skip it to keep them thick and domed.

payload.text: These stay pale — Splenda doesn't brown. Pull around the 12–15 min mark when the edges are just set and the center still looks underdone; waiting for any color means overbaked, dry centers.
The 10–15 min pan rest is where the centers finish cooking and proteins set. Moving them too soon means they fall apart; judging texture before fully cooled gives a false "gummy" read.

The optional pan-slap is the bakery 'pan-bang' trick: dropping the hot tray collapses the risen center so the cookie spreads thin with crackly, rippled edges and a chewier bite. Do it halfway and again out of the oven for the most spread; skip it entirely to keep these tall and gooey.
